diagrams.title: Social Platform Architecture Layers
diagrams.description: Shows the multi-layer architecture of a social platform with user-facing, application, caching, and data layers
diagrams.purpose: Understanding where capacity planning interventions occur at each layer

diagrams.title: Traffic Flow During Viral Event
diagrams.description: Demonstrates how traffic spikes propagate through the system during viral content events
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing flash crowd effect and system response

diagrams.title: API Request Flow with Rate Limiting
diagrams.description: Shows how API requests flow through the system with rate limiting applied
elements: If under limit → Forward to Service
diagrams.purpose: Understanding rate limiting mechanics and capacity protection

diagrams.title: Horizontal vs Vertical Scaling Comparison
diagrams.description: Visual comparison of vertical and horizontal scaling approaches
elements: Vertical: Small server → Big server (single point)
Load Balancer distributes to multiple nodes
No single point of failure in horizontal
diagrams.purpose: Choosing appropriate scaling strategy for social platforms

diagrams.title: CDN Distribution Network
diagrams.description: Shows how CDN caches content at geographic edge locations
elements: User in Asia → Asia CDN Node
User in Europe → Europe CDN Node
User in Americas → Americas CDN Node
Cache hit → Serve from nearest node
Reduction in origin server load visualization
diagrams.purpose: Understanding CDN benefits for global social platforms
